headline: 3 earnings to watch on Tuesday, July 21
summary: Asking for a Trend Host Josh Lipton tees up what investors should be on the lookout for on Tuesday, July 21, including earnings from General Motors Co. (GM), Northrop Grumman Co. (NOC), and D.R. Horton Inc (DHI).

headline: General Motors reports stronger than expected Q2 results, raises guidance
summary: GM reported its pre-tax profits for Q2 rose, but its net income was hit due to special charges related to EVs. CEO Barra said GM is on a good path.

content.title: General Motors reports stronger than expected Q2 results, raises guidance
content.summary: GM reported its pre-tax profits for Q2 rose, but its net income was hit due to special charges related to EVs. CEO Barra said GM is on a good path.

headline: GM releases 2026 second-quarter results, raises full-year 2026 guidance and declares quarterly dividend
summary: General Motors (NYSE: GM) today reported second-quarter 2026 revenue of $48.0 billion, net income attributable to stockholders of $1.3 billion, and EBIT-adjusted of $3.9 billion.

content.title: GM releases 2026 second-quarter results, raises full-year 2026 guidance and declares quarterly dividend
content.summary: General Motors (NYSE: GM) today reported second-quarter 2026 revenue of $48.0 billion, net income attributable to stockholders of $1.3 billion, and EBIT-adjusted of $3.9 billion.

headline: GM earnings & outlook impressed, thanks to lower tariffs. But don't get too comfortable.
summary: General Motors (GM) raised its 2026 outlook by $500 million, thanks in part to better margins and lower tariffs. Morning Brief host Julie Hyman discusses more with Yahoo Finance Breaking Business News Reporter Jake Conley and Payne Capital Management president Ryan Payne.

content.title: GM earnings & outlook impressed, thanks to lower tariffs. But don't get too comfortable.
content.description:
General Motors (GM) raised its 2026 outlook by $500 million, thanks in part to better margins and lower tariffs.

Morning Brief host Julie Hyman discusses more with Yahoo Finance Breaking Business News Reporter Jake Conley and Payne Capital Management president Ryan Payne.
content.summary: General Motors (GM) raised its 2026 outlook by $500 million, thanks in part to better margins and lower tariffs. Morning Brief host Julie Hyman discusses more with Yahoo Finance Breaking Business News Reporter Jake Conley and Payne Capital Management president Ryan Payne.

headline: GM Raises Guidance but Cuts Its Net Income Forecast Again
summary: North American margins drove the beat, yet the automaker lowered net income guidance for a second straight quarter.

headline: General Motors lifts 2026 outlook despite hit from electric retreat
summary: General Motors raised its full-year profit forecast on Tuesday after reporting solid quarterly results on strong vehicle pricing that offset a hit from costs tied to winding down electric vehicle investments.The big US automaker reported $1.3 billion in profits, down 31 percent from the year-ago period.

content.title: General Motors lifts 2026 outlook despite hit from electric retreat
content.summary: General Motors raised its full-year profit forecast on Tuesday after reporting solid quarterly results on strong vehicle pricing that offset a hit from costs tied to winding down electric vehicle investments.The big US automaker reported $1.3 billion in profits, down 31 percent from the year-ago period.

thumbnail.caption: General Motors lifted its profit outlook, citing strong pricing in North America where demand for pickup trucks and other large vehicles remains robust (JUSTIN SULLIVAN)

headline: GM’s Secret Weapon: Fewer Shares and Stronger Earnings
summary: General Motors  reported better-than-expected second-quarter earnings on Tuesday, with an assist from stock repurchases, sending the stock higher in premarket trading.  For the quarter, GM announced an adjusted operating profit of $3.9 billion from sales of $48 billion.  The total tariff impacts on GM remain significant, but GM has had a year to offset costs.

content.title: GM’s Secret Weapon: Fewer Shares and Stronger Earnings
content.summary: General Motors  reported better-than-expected second-quarter earnings on Tuesday, with an assist from stock repurchases, sending the stock higher in premarket trading.  For the quarter, GM announced an adjusted operating profit of $3.9 billion from sales of $48 billion.  The total tariff impacts on GM remain significant, but GM has had a year to offset costs.

headline: GM raises 2026 guidance after second-quarter earnings top expectations (GM)
summary: Automaker beats forecasts but shares move lowerGeneral Motors Co. (NYSE:GM) reported stronger-than-expected second-quarter results on Tuesday, surpassing Wall Street forecasts for both earnings and revenue while increasing its financial guidance for the second time this year.

content.summary: Automaker beats forecasts but shares move lowerGeneral Motors Co. (NYSE:GM) reported stronger-than-expected second-quarter results on Tuesday, surpassing Wall Street forecasts for both earnings and revenue while increasing its financial guidance for the second time this year.

headline: General Motors’s (NYSE:GM) Q2 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
summary: Automotive manufacturer General Motors (NYSE:GM) reported Q2 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 1.9% year on year to $48.03 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.57 per share was 12.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: General Motors’s (NYSE:GM) Q2 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
content.summary: Automotive manufacturer General Motors (NYSE:GM) reported Q2 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 1.9% year on year to $48.03 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.57 per share was 12.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.
